collaborations.title: Rheumatology and Immunology Lupus Erythematosus Agent-Assisted Diagnosis and Treatment Project
collaborations.partnerName: The Seventh Affiliated Hospital, Sun Yat-sen University
collaborations.team: Dr. Jieruo Gu's Team, Department of Rheumatology and Immunology, The Seventh Affiliated Hospital, Sun Yat-sen University
collaborations.intro: The laboratory focuses on the research and development of a Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE)-assisted diagnosis large model and its clinical application, and has launched cooperation with The Seventh Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University.
collaborations.description:
The laboratory focuses on the research and development of a Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE)-assisted diagnosis large model and its clinical application, and has launched cooperation with The Seventh Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University.
Aiming at the pain points of SLE, such as difficulties in early diagnosis and high misdiagnosis rate, the project integrates resources from both parties: The Seventh Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University provides more than 1,500 clinical cases per year (including severe cases and referral scenarios) to ensure the clinical authenticity of the data; relying on more than 5 years of follow-up multimodal data, it supplements information on the disease course dimension, so as to jointly build a compliant dataset of no less than 100,000 cases (with no less than 10,000 high-quality cases).
Technically, the project follows the LLM+Agent route. Clinical experts from The Seventh Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University (e.g., the team from the Department of Rheumatology and Immunology) participate in the definition of data labels, interpretation of model results, and guidance on medical translation, ensuring the model conforms to clinical diagnostic logic. The goal is to achieve an accuracy of no less than 93% and a recall rate of no less than 94% for the model on the test set within 5 months; within 6 months, complete the software prototype V1.0 and collect more than 10 pieces of trial feedback from doctors of The Seventh Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University.
